name: Monstera oreophila
aboutText: Monstera oreophila is a distinct climbing species native to the misty montane forests of Costa Rica and Panama. As a hemiepiphyte, it utilizes its adventitious roots to ascend tree trunks in the humid forest understory. It is celebrated for its elongated, elliptic leaves that feature elegant perforations as the plant reaches maturity. With its thick, matte to glossy foliage and characteristically long, tapering apices, it remains a highly sought-after specimen for aroid enthusiasts.

fieldNotes.title: Mountain-Loving Monstera
fieldNotes.author: Aroid Aaron
fieldNotes.content: Oreophila means 'mountain-loving,' which accurately describes a Costa Rican to Panamanian Monstera adapted to higher elevation forest conditions than most of its lowland relatives. Cooler temperatures, consistent cloud moisture, and higher light levels characterise the montane habitats this species occupies — which is relevant to UK cultivation since montane species frequently perform better in the British climate than their lowland relatives, due to the closer alignment with northern European seasonal temperature profiles. A useful and interesting plant for this reason.
